title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jane Olivia discusses the radical views of Howard Ricks with Hercule Poirot. She admits that she arranged for Ricks to make an appointment with her Uncle Archie at the Charlotte Street dentist office, hoping that Archie's kindness would mitigate Ricks's hatred toward him. However, Jane confesses she became fearful of the appointment because Ricks occasionally seeks "shortcuts" to achieve his goals.

Over a month after the death of the dentist Mr. Morley, Inspector Japp and Poirot discuss the disappearance of Miss Sainsbury-Sil. While Japp maintains that Morley committed suicide, Poirot believes the dentist was murdered. Japp notes that Miss Sainsbury-Sil was on the same ship as Ambrosio when returning from India, and a waiter reported that she had lunch with Ambrosio at the Savoy Hotel the week before his death. Japp mentions that Ambrosio was linked to espionage and Central Europeans, though he doubts Miss Sainsbury-Sil was involved in such activities. Poirot recalls that Reginald Barnes of Eling Castle Lodge had previously mentioned international organizations to him.

Japp reports that despite her description being published, no one has found Miss Sainsbury-Sil; various sightings in Yorkshire, Liverpool, Devon, and Ramsgate have proven to be false leads. Japp suggests that if Morley was murdered, he might have told Miss Sainsbury-Sil something that made the killer jealous, leading to her disappearance. Poirot concludes that such a crime would imply the involvement of a serious organization. After Japp departs, Poirot reflects on the information gathered, waiting for the final link to appear so he can organize his findings.
